title: Inbreeding depression in pearl millet composites
abstract:
The objective of this investigation was to examine the effects of one generation
of selfing on grain yield, plant height, head length, 1000 seed weight, and days to
flowering in the base populations as well as in three successive full sib recurrem
selection cycle bulks of the Medium Maturity Composite and Serere Composite-l of
pearl millet -[Penniseium americanum (L.) Leeke). The experimental materials consisted
of random mated bulks · and single generation selfed bulks from 400 random
pl~nts corresponding to each cycle (cycle 0 to 3) of both the composites. Results
showed that a.verage inbreeding depressions in both the composites were highest for
grain yield (25 % and 36 %) followed by 1000 seed weight (L2 % and 14 0/0). Plant height,days to flowering and --head length were least affected...............
